by subtracting 16 minutes from this figure. Then you get the
M. C. which is equal to 3'1-10c-27'-10*. In this example you
find both the ascending degree and the M. C. degree to be the
same. It need not be the same. If you work out the ascendant
and the M. C. for a male birth at Delhi on 10th December
1962 at 9-05 A. M. 1. S. T., you will find that the figure for the
former is 8s-21*-36'-9 * and for the latter 6^-8 '-10'-35'. This
would show that the houses are not of equal length. Let us
now try to get the figures for the remaining ten houses. For
this purpose we have to work out first the 11th and 12th houses.
This is done as follows :—Subtract the figure of the M. C. from

26 FUNDAMENTALS OF ASTROLOGY
that of the ascendant. Divide the result by 3. Then add the
quotient to the figure of the 10th house (M. C.) to get the figure
of the ilth house. If you add the quotient again to the figure
of the 11th house, you will get that of the J2th house. By
